title: damburneya smithii
intro: Damburneya smithii, also known as the Smith's dammar or Borneo rosewood, is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical forests of Borneo. The bark and heartwood of Damburneya smithii have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which help to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Damburneya smithii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Damburneya smithii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and wounds.
